title: loxotrema tuberosa
intro: Loxotrema tuberosa, also known as Mexican prickly poppy, is a plant species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Loxotrema tuberosa include relieving pain and reducing inflammation, as well as treating skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Herbal preparations of Loxotrema tuberosa typically involve making a tea from the plant's roots or leaves, or using it in topical creams and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
